nativeRange.habitat: Rocky subtidal environments, reef-associated zones; tends to tolerate shadier or deeper positions within the photic zone

decorPreferences: shaded or lower-light areas of display

imageCandidates.notes: Primary product photo from AlgaeBarn Leafy Sphere Ogo listing
Product photo from Mosaic Macros Purple Dragon's Breath (H. maculata) listing

summary: Leafy Ogo (Halymenia maculata) is a red macroalgae with broad, blotchy blades in shades of pink, red, and purple. It tends to do well in shaded or lower-light areas of the display and grows slowly compared to most other export macroalgae. Herbivorous fish such as tangs are known to graze on it readily.
careNotes:
Leafy Ogo (Halymenia maculata) is a red macroalgae in the family Halymeniaceae, order Halymeniales — the same family as the closely related Dragon's Breath (H. durvillei) and Dragon's Tongue (H. dilatata). Its relatively thick, broadly lobed blades display a distinctively blotchy coloration ranging from pink through red to purple; the blade edges may be slightly frilly but are typically broader and stiffer than the filamentous Ogo species it is sometimes confused with. This species is sometimes sold under the trade name 'Purple Dragon's Breath' and is noted as one of the few genuinely purple-looking macroalgae available in the hobby.

TRADE NAME NOTE: The 'Ogo' trade name is shared across two unrelated genera — Gracilaria spp. (Red Ogo, Green Ogo, Thick Ogo, and others) and Halymenia spp. (Leafy Ogo and related species). Confirming the scientific name before purchase is advisable, as Gracilaria Ogos tend to be faster-growing and more branched, while Halymenia Ogos are broad-bladed and slow-growing. 'Purple Dragon's Breath' should also not be confused with Dragon's Breath, which is a different species (H. durvillei).

This species is regarded as VERY sensitive to shipping and is generally recommended to be transported via overnight delivery to ensure live arrival.

Generally advised to maintain temperatures around 25.6–28.3°C (78–83°F), pH 7.8–8.4, SG 1.023–1.025, and alkalinity 8–12 dKH. Moderate to high water flow is typically recommended. Leafy Ogo tends to grow either free-floating or holdfast-attached; maximum diameter has been reported around 18 cm (7 in). Herbivorous fish — including tangs and rabbitfish — and sea urchins have been known to consume it readily, making it useful as a supplemental grazing food but a poor fit for tanks with dedicated herbivores.

As a photosynthetic organism, Leafy Ogo does not require direct feeding; trace element supplementation may support coloration in some systems. Propagation in the aquarium hobby is primarily by fragmentation.

ATTACHMENT WARNING: As a red alga, this species will MELT at the base if buried in sand. It should be attached to rock using superglue, zip-tie or fishing line, or wedging rather than placed on a sand bed. Among macroalgae, sand-bed placement is generally only suited to green algae such as Caulerpa, Halimeda, Penicillus, Rhipocephalus, and Udotea, as well as some calcified Dasycladales.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros
additional.notes: Confirmed species as H. maculata; noted extreme shipping sensitivity requiring overnight shipping for live arrival guarantee; described as 'one of the only true purple-looking species in the hobby'. Limited husbandry parameter data on product page.

additional.name: Wikipedia — Halymenia
additional.notes: Confirmed genus taxonomy: family Halymeniaceae, order Halymeniales, class Florideophyceae. H. maculata listed as a recognized species (J. Agardh). AlgaeBase was inaccessible (CAPTCHA) during this research pass.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros general macroalgae care guide
additional.notes: Slice-wide secondary source — provides authoritative Mosaic-wide parameter targets (temp 22-26°C, pH 8.1-8.4, Ca 400-450 ppm, Mg 1250-1350 ppm, NO3 5-20 ppm, PO4 0.02-0.1 ppm) and the 4-method propagation standard. Added retroactively 2026-06-08.
